Grammar","subtitle":"Fill in the blanks.","icon":"📝","color":"text-[oklch(0.55_0.16_60)]","bgColor":"bg-[oklch(0.95_0.04_60)]","description":"There are 10 blanks, numbered 21 to 30, in the passage below. From the list of words given, choose the most suitable word for each blank. Write the letter (A to Q) in the blank. The letters (I) and (O) have been omitted to avoid confusion during marking.","questions":[{"id":21,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"although"},{"id":22,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"only"},{"id":23,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"whose"},{"id":24,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"whether"},{"id":25,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"as"},{"id":26,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"being"},{"id":27,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"had"},{"id":28,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"despite"},{"id":29,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"which"},{"id":30,"type":"fill-blank","correctAnswer":"only"}],"grammarPassage":"Education plays a vital role in shaping individuals and societies. (21)___________ many people believe that academic success depends mainly on intelligence, research shows that motivation and effort are equally important. Students often perform well not (22)___________ because they are naturally gifted, but because they develop effective learning habits. Supportive environments also matter. Families (23)___________ attitudes towards education are positive tend to raise children who value learning. In contrast, students may struggle in situations (24)___________ encouragement is lacking, even if they have strong potential. Studies suggest that success in education is closely linked to (25)___________ learners believe about their own abilities. Teachers can influence this by recognising effort, (26)___________ confidence gradually through constructive feedback. However, real improvement depends on (27)___________ students are willing to reflect on mistakes and learn from them. (28)___________ examinations remain an important part of most education systems, they cannot fully measure creativity or critical thinking. A learner (29)___________ curiosity is encouraged is more likely to become independent than one who studies only to pass tests. Ultimately, education is not (30)___________ a pathway to employment, but a lifelong process that shapes how people understand the world.","wordBank":[{"letter":"A","word":"although"},{"letter":"B","word":"which"},{"letter":"C","word":"despite"},{"letter":"D","word":"has"},{"letter":"E","word":"whose"},{"letter":"F","word":"being"},{"letter":"G","word":"as"},{"letter":"H","word":"what"},{"letter":"J","word":"only"},{"letter":"K","word":"where"},{"letter":"L","word":"whether"},{"letter":"M","word":"while"},{"letter":"N","word":"had"},{"letter":"P","word":"despite"}]},{"id":"comprehension","title":"Part 3: Comprehension","subtitle":"Refer to the passage below when you answer questions 31 to 40.","icon":"📚","color":"text-[oklch(0.55_0.16_260)]","bgColor":"bg-[oklch(0.95_0.04_260)]","description":"Read the story and answer the questions that follow.","questions":[{"id":31,"type":"open-ended","question":"Based on paragraph 1, what did the narrator do once a year?","answer":"The narrator went to the dentist for an annual check-up."},{"id":32,"type":"open-ended","question":"Give two reasons why Mother would not be able to get home on time to cook dinner because of the heavy rain.","subQuestions":["a","b"],"answer":"a) It was raining heavily / It had started to pour. b) There were no taxis available as most of them were occupied."},{"id":33,"type":"true-false","statements":["a) The narrator and Mother went to the café in the morning.","b) It had been raining before the narrator and Mother left the house.","c) Mother believed that one had to be wary of men who were excessively nice."],"trueFalseStatements":["a) False","b) False","c) True"],"reasons":["They went there in the afternoon / 'Yesterday afternoon'.","It was a hot, sweltering day when they left the house.","Mother had a golden rule that the nicer a man seemed, the more suspicious one should be."]},{"id":34,"type":"open-ended","question":"The old man said that he was 'in a bit of a predicament'. Explain clearly what the predicament was.","answer":"He had left his wallet at home and had no money to take a taxi home."},{"id":35,"type":"table","question":"Based on the information, complete the following table.","rows":["situation","thought","action"],"tableData":["The old man offered his umbrella for five dollars.","Mother had doubts about the old man's intentions.","She gave the old man five dollars."],"tableData2":["Mother spotted the old man enter the café.","Mother suspected that the old man might not be telling the truth / doubted his intentions.","Mother followed him to the café."]},{"id":36,"type":"open-ended","question":"The narrator asked Mother why she had initially been so suspicious of the man. What had Mother done earlier to make her ask that question?","answer":"Mother tightened her grip on the narrator's hand and drew the narrator closer to her while looking at the man suspiciously."},{"id":37,"type":"reference","question":"What do these words refer to?","items":["it (line 7)","them (line 8)","she (line 33)"],"answers":["the rain / the weather","the taxis","the woman the old man spoke to"]},{"id":38,"type":"order","question":"Write 1, 2, and 3 in the blanks below to indicate the order in which the events occurred in the story.","events":["The narrator noticed that the old man was carrying a nice umbrella.","The narrator and Mother witnessed the old man take an umbrella before walking out of the door.","The narrator and Mother saw a woman with an umbrella."],"correctOrder":[1,2,3]},{"id":39,"type":"phrase","question":"Look at the table below. Complete the table by identifying the correct word/phrase in the passage.","items":["Which three-word phrase tells you that the narrator and Mother did not expect to see the old man again?","Which three-word phrase tells you that Mother finally knew the old man had an ulterior motive?"],"answers":["to our surprise","his little game"]},{"id":40,"type":"checkbox","question":"Based on the passage, which two of the following words best describe the old man? Put a tick (✔) in the line beside each of your answers. DO NOT TICK MORE THAN TWO BOXES.","options":["devious","reliable","gullible","thoughtful","clever","honest"],"correctAnswers":[0,4]}],"passage":"Yesterday afternoon, Mother took me to the dentist for my annual check-up. After that, we went to a café where Mother allowed me to get a slice of cake for a treat while she had a cup of coffee. While we were having our tea, dark clouds gathered ominously outside. By the time we went out of the café the weather had taken a turn for the worse. It had started to pour! We were not prepared as it had been a hot, sweltering day when we had left the house earlier. We stood outside the café watching sheets of rain coming down.

"It looks like it isn't going to stop. I'll be late cooking dinner," Mother murmured. We moved onto the pavement in the pouring rain, looking for a taxi. Lots of them were occupied and went by without stopping. Just then, a kind looking old man came up to us. He looked like he was about my grandfather's age. He gave a slight bow as he said to Mother, "Excuse me, I was wondering if I could ask a small favour of you." I noticed, with some envy, that he was holding a large umbrella over him, keeping him dry. I saw Mother looking at him suspiciously as she nodded her head, tightened her grip on my hand and drew me closer to her. "With strange men," she had a golden rule which said that the nicer a man seemed to be, the more suspicious you must become of him. However, this old man seemed like a real gentleman. He was very polite, well-dressed and well-spoken.

"The truth of the matter is, I am in a bit of a predicament. I've left my wallet at home and I have no money for a taxi home!" the old man said.

"Are you asking me for money?" Mother interrupted.

Mortified, the man exclaimed, "Oh no! I apologise. Let me get to the point. I would like to offer you my umbrella if you could give me five dollars. As you can see, it is a very nice and new one! I walked a little too far today and I don't think my old legs could take the walk back!"

Upon hearing that, I could see that Mother was beginning to melt. Finally, she reached into her bag, took out the money and gave it to him but I could see that she still had her doubts. He bowed as he thanked my Mother and walked off. Seeing we now had an umbrella, we decided to walk home. As we walked, I asked Mother why she had initially been so suspicious of him. She told me she was being cautious as she was not sure what his intentions were but she was glad she had been able to help in the end. He had been, in her opinion, a real gentleman.

A short while later, to our surprise, we spotted the same man and he did not look very tired nor did he look like he was trying to catch a taxi either! Curious, we watched as he hurried into a busy café where he appeared to peruse the menu before turning to leave. As he left, he surreptitiously took one of the umbrellas from the umbrella stand and walked out the door! We decided to follow him to see what he was up to. We did not have to wait long before we saw him stop to talk to a woman who was in a situation similar to what we had been in earlier. After a while, she gave him some money and in return, he gave her the umbrella before walking away quickly.

"Did you see that! So, that's his little game!" Mother shrieked."},{"id":"writing","title":"Part 4: Writing","subtitle":"Write a composition of about 200-250 words.","icon":"✍️","color":"text-[oklch(0.55_0.16_330)]","bgColor":"bg-[oklch(0.95_0.04_330)]","description":"Write about an unexpected encounter that changed the way you looked at someone or something.","questions":[{"id":1,"type":"writing","topic":"An Unexpected Encounter","instructions":"Write about an unexpected encounter that changed the way you looked at someone or something. You may include:","wordCount":"200-250 words","prompts":["where and when the encounter happened","who you met","what happened during the encounter","how your thoughts or feelings changed afterwards"]}]}]
